name: Captain Harl
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, battle-worn guard captain in lamellar armor, torchlit gatehouse, rain droplets, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: You brought it back? Saints. The camp will howl when they notice.

nodes.text:
We can show the banner to the people—raise morale.

Or keep it secret—use it as bait, or as proof only when needed.

Choose. Either choice has teeth.

choices.label: Hold a public reveal (morale surge, enemy adapts).
Keep it secret (tactical advantage, less morale).

name: Councilor Vayne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, stern councilor with wax seals and maps, crystal glow, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: The enemy loses coordination when their symbols die. Good.

nodes.text:
The Great Assault is coming within the day.

We’ll issue emergency authority.

And we’ll decide whether the banner becomes hope—or becomes a knife.

name: Archivist Salenne
portraitPrompt: fantasy character portrait, mage archivist with crystal ball light, chalk runes, painterly, no text

dialogue.greeting: The ball shows a single, clean truth: the next assault is their biggest.

nodes.text:
They will push three fronts:

1) A siege tower at the main wall.
2) A ram at the gate.
3) A drains surge—if the ‘second mouth’ still lives.

If you choose correctly, thousands live.

If not, the city becomes a corridor.

choices.label: Ask how to counter-signal under pressure.
Ask how to blunt the drains surge.
